join_order.notes: sic_ok bitset over 86K sub rows; direct lookup via num.sub_fk
CRITICAL: pre-size hash table to ~51K (EQ rows filtered by sic_ok), NOT 1.24M. Two-pass: first count qualifying rows, then allocate and insert. Reduces memory from 40MB to ~1.6MB (fits L3).
Use tag_pk_index for O(1) lookup; check abstract[tag_row]==0 inline

data_structures.pre_eq_hashset: compact_hash_map(key=(uint32_t sub_fk, uint32_t tag_code, uint32_t version_code), value=uint32_t count, pre_sized=COUNTED_QUALIFYING_ROWS ~51K). CRITICAL: two-pass build — first iterate EQ range counting entries where sic_ok[sub_fk], then allocate table to 2× that count, then re-iterate and insert. Memory: ~51K × 2 × 16 bytes = 1.6MB, fits in L3.
data_structures.aggregation: thread-local compact_hash_map(key=uint64_t(sic,tag_row), value=(double sum, uint64_t count, vector<int32_t> cik_buf), INITIAL_CAPACITY=4096 per thread). 64 threads × 4096 × 32 bytes = 8MB total, fits in L3. Use small_vector or inline std::vector<int32_t> for CIK collection — no unordered_set.

parallelism.notes: Thread-local agg maps with SMALL initial capacity (4096, not 100K). Grows on demand. Total init memory = 8MB vs previous 384MB.

indexes.purpose: get EQ row range in pre

aggregation.group_key: (int16_t sic, uint32_t tag_row) packed as uint64_t
aggregation.group_key_notes: p.stmt is constant 'EQ', excluded from key. tag_row is used as proxy for tlabel; re-aggregate by actual tlabel string only at output (small final map).

accumulators.cik_buf: std::vector<int32_t> — append CIK values during scan, sort+unique only at merge/output. Avoids per-insert hash set overhead.

aggregation.merge_strategy: iterate local maps, merge into global map. For CIK: use std::move to transfer vectors from largest contributor, then append+sort+unique from remaining. Or: concatenate all cik_bufs per group, do single sort+unique at the end.

optimization_notes: Category C (work elimination). Root cause: massive over-allocation of data structures. (1) pre_eq hash table was pre-sized to 1.24M (40MB) but only ~51K entries after SIC filter — fix with two-pass counting to right-size to 1.6MB (fits L3). (2) Thread-local AggMaps pre-sized to 100K each = 384MB across 64 threads, causing ~150ms of page faults on initialization — reduce to 4096 initial capacity = 8MB total (fits L3). (3) unordered_set<int32_t> per aggregation group for CIK tracking has huge per-instance overhead and slow merge — replace with vector<int32_t> + deferred sort+unique, which is faster for the typical case of few distinct CIKs per group. These three changes eliminate ~250ms of wasted memory initialization and reduce merge from 58ms by removing hash set iteration overhead.
